rulings.Case Title: GRADY FRAZIER VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder In Defendant Oscar E. Erickson, Inc.S Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Prosecute Claim
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 7, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Honeywell International Inc.¿s joinder in Oscar E. Erickson, Inc.¿s motion to dismiss for failure to prosecute pursuant to C.C.P. 583.410-583.430 is denied as untimely. Defendant failed to provide proper notice as required by CRC 3.1342(a) and C.C.P. 1005(b). The joinder is denied without prejudice to defendant filing its own motion and providing proper notice to Mr. Frazier. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT ROSS et al VS. C.C. MOORE & CO. ENGINEERS
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 7, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant D. Zelinsky & Sons, Inc.¿s motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issue 1 (strict liability) and denied as to issue 2 (premise owner/contractor liability). As to issue 1, although plaintiffs represent that they will dismiss their strict liability cause of action, no such dismissal has been filed. As to issue 2, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs cannot prevail on their premises owner/contractor liability cause of action because it failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs can only prevail on this cause of action if they can show that defendant owned, leased, managed or controlled any relevant sites or that defendant failed to warn Mr. Ross of a dangerous condition or exercised control over a premise. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES SHIFFER et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Granting Motion For Summary Judgment (Ccp § 1008 (A)); And-Or In The Alternative, Motion For New Trial (Ccp § 657) As To Defendant Cbs Corporation (Fka Viacom Inc., Fka Westinghouse Electric Corporation)
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 7, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Plaintiffs¿ motion for reconsideration pursuant to C.C.P. 1008(a) of order granting CBS Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for new trial pursuant to C.C.P. 657 as to CBS Corp.: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:45am. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)
